  "findings": [
    "Severe belt mistracking caused by off-center loading and misaligned/skewed idlers",
    "Potential structural contact, material spillage, and friction/fire hazard from mistracked belt",
    "Inadequate guarding or lack of emergency pull-cords in the immediate zone of the mistracking belt"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Lock out and tag out (LOTO) the conveyor system immediately before attempting any physical adjustments",
    "Re-align and adjust the skewed idlers to correct the belt path",
    "Adjust the chute or loading mechanism to ensure material is loaded centrally onto the belt",
    "Inspect the belt edges and structure for friction damage prior to restarting"
  ],
  "rationale": "The conveyor belt is actively mistracking due to a combination of off-center loading and skewed idlers. Operating a mistracked belt poses severe safety risks, including friction-induced fires, structural damage, material spillage hazards, and potential entanglement if workers attempt to adjust it while running. Immediate shutdown and corrective maintenance under LOTO are required.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.212 (General requirements for all machines / guarding)",
    "CEMA (Conveyor Equipment Manufacturers Association) Safety Standards"
  ]
}
